channel.title: Posts on YiSheng's secret garden
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on YiSheng's secret garden

item.title: May 16th — A Midsummer Day in Douliu
item.description:
slug: writting

Regressing to a Primitive State

While doing my literature review, I realized my writing and argumentation had degraded to the point where I needed to type out fragmented notes and ask AI to assemble them into sentences for me. I would read them, understand them, then go back and revise.

item.title: "Lost Love Front Alliance" Is Music from Thailand!
item.description:
slug: gress-bus_en

Childhood memories flipped!!!!!!

Today I am talking about music with Zhu Sirui (my Thai friend) It turns out that the song “Lost Love Front Alliance” that I heard since I was a kid is a cover. The original song is in Thai!!!

item.title: A Letter from a High School Friend
item.description:
slug: friend-plus7_en

Letter from +7

2026.05.07 she said

What I want to say is Every time I see you convert your ideals into action, it drives me forward. It’s amazing. We obviously don’t contact each other very often. So you have always been a friend that I am very proud of. You will attract equally good people as you said in your writing. Like me :D)

item.title: ROC Year 115, May 8th
item.description:
Read this today https://alicehsu.blog/zh-tw/posts/2026/02/the-apothecary-diaries/ I also think about how I have some very good seniors. I don’t care about being a roll king But be good to your friends and the things you like too

I was particularly impressed by these during my master’s degree

item.title: Adventure-Type Player
item.description:
slug: adventure-time_en

Adventure gamers

I like to jokingly call myself an adventure player. Because I look back carefully at my life path It doesn’t matter

Data processing department, carpentry, direct layout instructor, Thai\Spanish, VR developer, part-time assistant in the electrical department Entrepreneurship, German, now a master’s student at a design institute, diving enthusiast, English,,,

item.title: Senior Lab Mates Share Their Design School Experience
item.description:
slug: exp-senior-design

The Technical Crossroads

Today I asked my senior lab mates: “I feel stuck at a crossroads — one step back is device interaction, one step forward is artificial intelligence. How did you figure out which of the thousands of papers you actually wanted to study?”

item.title: Unprecedented Freedom
item.description:
slug: deletesocialmedia_en

disappear?

When I press delete fb/ig account Representative I lost 78+ of my close friends Photos and videos from nearly 9 years A bunch of likes following accounts

I’m realizing I play ig late stage after 2024 I almost manage the personality Not as simple and relaxed as before Just the pressure to publish

item.title: What is RSS? A Tool Everyone Should Know About
item.description:
slug: rss

What’s RSS

RSS stands for Really Simple Syndication. In short, it extracts web content and turns it into an XML file, allowing users to subscribe to and read all their favorite sources in one place using an RSS Feed along with an RSS reader.

item.title: Graduate Tools — Tips from Experience
item.description:
slug: exp-seminar

You can find past papers from a conference you’re interested in, right-click and print the page as a PDF for a bunch of paper titles, then give it to Claude to see if any match your topic. You can also ask Perplexity to search for related ones. Otherwise, look for papers with similar problem statements.

You can first use Gemini to extract summary keywords — 10 from an engineering perspective and 10 from a design perspective, then organize the most important ones. After the analysis, give it the full text for a more accurate result. For the filtered papers, you don’t have to translate them one by one yourself.

item.title: Ivan Said — No Two Leaves in the World Are Exactly the Same
item.description:
slug: stay-alive

The Birth of Life

Once a year, pink coral spawns on the weekend — 05.09.26. Late at night, I saw this news in the group chat and a thought suddenly came to me:

I want to go witness the birth of life, because it is truly beautiful.

item.title: Academic Achievements
item.description:
slug: history-prize

Education

National Yunlin University of Science and Technology, NYUST M.S. in Digital Media Design - Current

National Yunlin University of Science and Technology, NYUST B.S. in Information Management - Graduated

Seminar

  • The 9th IEEE International Conference on Multimedia Information Processing and Retrieval(IEEE MIPR), special session

item.title: Things I Learned as Staff at an International Conference
item.description:
Do Your Best, and Always Ask: “Is There Anything I Can Help With?”

These are the things I learned:

  • Actively participate in the conference and offer timely assistance to increase interaction and visibility
  • Always carry your laptop and stay present in the office to show commitment and professionalism
  • Build familiarity through consistent attendance and daily conversations, laying the groundwork for future collaboration
  • Prepare business cards to help expand your network
  • Even when your contribution seems limited, keep showing up — trust accumulates over time
  • Stay open to asking questions and learning from others
  • Have regular weekly check-ins with your mentor to maintain a steady feedback loop

item.title: When Discipline Becomes Routine: Preparing for CAADRIA Two Months Out, I Chose to Sync with the World
item.description:
slug: prep-CAADRIA-2026

When Discipline Becomes Routine: Preparing for CAADRIA Two Months Out, I Chose to Sync with the World

Translation Is Not Enough

Preparatory study to reduce the cognitive gap. Many people say, “Just use body language or a translation app to chat.” But having managed cross-national team collaboration before, I know that is far from enough. Pronunciation, regional language isolation (different countries have different vocabulary preferences — even the same language can be incomprehensible), and beyond that, one-to-one translation wastes so much time.

item.title: After Experiencing the Silent Darkness of the Deep Sea, I Realized What the Meaning of Life Is!
item.description:
slug: 0421

After Experiencing the Silent Darkness of the Deep Sea, I Realized What the Meaning of Life Is!

Before the boat dive, I couldn’t quite feel it — Why am I here? What am I supposed to be doing here? Why am I confining myself to NYUST?

item.title: Writing My Own Story
item.description:
slug: Story

Writing My Own Story

I believe life is less about finding yourself and more about creating yourself. At 20, I transitioned from seeing life through a lens of limitation to embracing the role of the protagonist in my own journey. This shift sparked a decade of entrepreneurship, international collaboration, and a relentless pursuit of excellence.

item.title: Building a Highly Customized AI Assistant with AnythingLLM - Tutorial
item.description:
slug: llm-rag-AnythingLLM

What is AnythingLLM

AnythingLLM is a powerful full-stack AI application that can convert multiple document formats, resources, or content into text that large language models (LLMs) can use for querying.

This tool not only supports multi-user management (yes, it effectively comes with a built-in admin panel), but also offers a variety of LLM and vector database choices, making it an excellent choice for building private, customized RAG solutions. Essentially, this toolkit can dramatically lower the development cost of RAG, and since it uses the MIT License, you can even modify and commercialize it directly.

item.title: AnythingLLM Installation — MacOS
item.description:
slug: llm-rag-docker-2

How to Install AnythingLLM on MacOS

Step 1: Install Homebrew (if not already installed)

PLAINTEXT 
/bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"
brew doctor
/bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)" brew doctor

Step 2: Install Docker Desktop for Mac

PLAINTEXT 
brew install --cask docker
open -a Docker # Launch Docker Desktop; first launch may require manual authorization
brew install --cask docker open -a Docker # Launch Docker Desktop; first launch may require manual authorization

After launching, wait for the Docker whale icon in the menu bar to turn green, indicating a successful start. Verify the installation:

item.title: AnythingLLM Installation — Windows
item.description:
slug: llm-rag-docker

Windows Install

PLAINTEXT 
WSL (Windows Subsystem for Linux) is a feature developed by Microsoft that allows users to natively run Linux binary executables on Windows 10/11 or Windows Server without needing a virtual machine (VM) or dual-boot setup.
WSL (Windows Subsystem for Linux) is a feature developed by Microsoft that allows users to natively run Linux binary executables on Windows 10/11 or Windows Server without needing a virtual machine (VM) or dual-boot setup.

Installing Docker via WSL (Ubuntu 22.04)

Step 1: Check WSL Version

PLAINTEXT 
wsl -v
wsl -v

Step 2: Install Ubuntu 22.04 LTS

PLAINTEXT 
wsl --install -d Ubuntu-22.04
wsl --install -d Ubuntu-22.04

After the installation is complete, you will be prompted to create a UNIX user account and set a username.

item.title: I feel like I have lost control over myself. What can i do?
item.description:
slug: always-crazy

Success comes from pain.

You’ve been wasting time because you’ve been avoiding pain.

There’s no secret shortcut to success.

The only thing that produces real results is to work.

Here’s the question you should ask yourself right now:

item.title: Yes, I Once Built a Startup
item.description:
slug: a-message-from-founder

Yes, I Once Built a Startup

At the end of 2024, we presented our idea at Microsoft
and stepped into our first real journey.
We visited local shops, spoke with users, and interviewed schools.
We collected meaningful data.
And the adventure began. More detail

item.title: The Seven Circles of Meeting Hell
item.description:
slug: oreilly-A-Day-in-the-Life-of-a-Senior-Leader

The Seven Circles of Meeting Hell

First Circle: THE SLOPPY AND THE UNPREPARED Meetings start late and run over. Attendees have not read the pre-supplied material, so we spend most of the time answering questions we had already answered elsewhere.

item.title: Beyond Vibe Coding
item.description:
slug: oreilly-Beyond-Vibe-Coding

Beyond Vibe Coding - Introduction

What Is Vibe Coding?

Vibe coding is a prompt-first, conversational way of building software.
You describe what you want; the AI writes the code.

item.title: Responsible Software Engineering
item.description:
slug: oreilly-Responsible-Software-Engineering

This book is written based on the author’s observations of colleagues at Google.
Disclaimer: This is a personal summary and reflection, not a substitute for the original book.

What Is Responsible Software Engineering?

Definition

Responsible software engineering means developing software products that benefit society while avoiding harm to the planet and its inhabitants.

item.title: Solitude Is a One-Person Celebration; Celebration Is a Crowd’s Loneliness
item.description:
slug: loneless-and-crowd

Solitude Is a One-Person Celebration; Celebration Is a Crowd’s Loneliness

Idolization

Yes—
I never really had idols growing up.

item.title: A Brief Reflection on Personal Heroism
item.description:
slug: hero-self

A Brief Reflection on Personal Heroism

In today’s world—whether in teamwork or society—it is inevitable to encounter “liability teammates.”
For those who have the capability to lift a team, improve efficiency, or shoulder responsibility,
a sense of personal heroism naturally emerges:
“If others are too slow, I’ll do it myself.”
“If I don’t step in, the whole thing collapses.”

item.title: The 22nd International conference on Cooperative Design Visualization and Engineering
item.description:
From Cooperation to Emotion: Reflections on CDVE 2025

The 22nd International Conference on Cooperative Design, Visualization, and Engineering (CDVE 2025) will take place in Bangkok, Thailand, from October 19–22, 2025.
This year’s theme centers on “cooperation” — not only between systems, but between people, ideas, and disciplines.

item.title: Carbon Emissions, Carbon Taxes, and Class - Who Is Paying for Whose Emissions?
item.description:
slug: Carbon-Emissions

Carbon Emissions, Carbon Taxes, and Class: Who Is Paying for Whose Emissions?

The global decarbonization movement is no longer just an environmental issue; it has become a central force shaping economic and social structures. While corporations hire consultants to optimize emission strategies or purchase carbon credits to reduce tax burdens, ordinary people silently bear the rising costs of electricity, fuel, and everyday living. Carbon emissions are becoming a new form of class divide.

item.title: linux_build_safety_wordpress
item.description:
slug: linux-build-safety-wordpress

Building a Secure WordPress Site on Linux

  1. Security Group
  2. Ports: 20, 21, 22, 80, 443

Setting up the Ubuntu environment:

Introduction

Simply put, Pandas is like the spreadsheet software we use in everyday life.

First, you need to import the module to use it:

item.title: Pico
item.description:
slug: Pico

Raspberry Pico

  1. Use Thonny to flash the blink (built-in LED blink) program onto the Pico.
  2. Use the Pico + 1602 LCD to display temperature data read from the built-in temperature sensor.
  3. Use a Raspberry Pi to flash/debug Pico programs (all steps).
  • getTemp.py
  • Temperature reading program
PYTHON= 
import utime
from machine import I2C, Pin, ADC
from lcd_api import LcdApi
from pico_i2c_lcd import I2cLcd
I2C_ADDR = 63
I2C_NUM_ROWS = 2
I2C_NUM_COLS = 16

i2c = I2C(0, sda=Pin(0), scl=Pin(1), freq=400000)
lcd = I2cLcd(i2c, I2C_ADDR, I2C_NUM_ROWS, I2C_NUM_COLS)
def read_temp():
 sensor_temp = ADC(4)
 conversion_factor = 3.3 / (65535)
 reading = sensor_temp.read_u16() * conversion_factor 
 temperature = 27 - (reading - 0.706)/0.001721
 formatted_temperature = "{:.2f}".format(temperature)
 string_temperature = str("Temp:" + formatted_temperature)
 print(string_temperature)
 utime.sleep(2)
 return string_temperature
while True:
 temperature = read_temp()
 lcd.move_to(0,0)
 lcd.putstr(temperature)
import utime from machine import I2C, Pin, ADC from lcd_api import LcdApi from pico_i2c_lcd import I2cLcd I2C_ADDR = 63 I2C_NUM_ROWS = 2 I2C_NUM_COLS = 16 i2c = I2C(0, sda=Pin(0), scl=Pin(1), freq=400000) lcd = I2cLcd(i2c, I2C_ADDR, I2C_NUM_ROWS, I2C_NUM_COLS) def read_temp(): sensor_temp = ADC(4) conversion_factor = 3.3 / (65535) reading = sensor_temp.read_u16() * conversion_factor temperature = 27 - (reading - 0.706)/0.001721 formatted_temperature = "{:.2f}".format(temperature) string_temperature = str("Temp:" + formatted_temperature) print(string_temperature) utime.sleep(2) return string_temperature while True: temperature = read_temp() lcd.move_to(0,0) lcd.putstr(temperature)
  1. Use VSCode with the Pico to develop MicroPython programs.

item.title: git
item.description:
slug: git

git

Introduction to Git - Brief History, Reasons for Use, and What is Version Control?

  • History: Released under the GPL license in 2005, created by Linus Torvalds.
  • Reason for use: When collaborating as a team, using git commands enables real-time updates without the hassle of the traditional method of uploading to the cloud and then downloading again.
  • Version control: Software that helps track code changes over a period of time.

Explanation of Version Control Logic

item.title: google_sheet_database
item.description:
slug: google-sheet-database

How to Use Google Sheets / Excel as a Database?

  1. Create a Google Spreadsheet
  2. Set up dummy data fields (field names must be in English):
  • id: serial number
  • name: full name
  • image: image
  • email: email address
  • phone: phone number

Note: the form referred to below means (Sheet 1), not DB

item.title: Three.js
item.description:
slug: Three-js

A Brief Introduction to Three.js

Three.js is an open-source JavaScript library built on top of the WebGL API. It wraps the complex operations of WebGL into easy-to-use APIs, making it convenient for developers to create 3D scenes and animations in web browsers.

item.title: gcp_02
item.description:
slug: gcp-02

GCP Sprint

  1. Load Balancer
  2. GCP Tutorial https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLvYsUzEf9kaP39bS21SKn4Onp3KQRbFsA
PLAINTEXT 
#! /bin/bash
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install -y python3 python3-dev python3-pip
cd nsc-q2
export EXTERNAL_IP=34.110.254.140	
sed -i "s/EXTERNAL_IP/${EXTERNAL_IP}/I"' ./templates/index.html
pip3 install -r requirements.txt
export GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=
GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS 
export DB_HOST= 
export DB_PORT= 
export DB_USER=
export DB_PASS=
export DB_NAME=test-q2-schema
export FLASK_APP=main.py
flask run -h 0.0.0.0 -p 80 &
#! /bin/bash sudo apt-get update sudo apt-get install -y python3 python3-dev python3-pip cd nsc-q2 export EXTERNAL_IP=34.110.254.140 sed -i "s/EXTERNAL_IP/${EXTERNAL_IP}/I"' ./templates/index.html pip3 install -r requirements.txt export GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS= GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS export DB_HOST= export DB_PORT= export DB_USER= export DB_PASS= export DB_NAME=test-q2-schema export FLASK_APP=main.py flask run -h 0.0.0.0 -p 80 &

Task 1: Build a Static Website with Nginx + VPC + Security Group

  1. Create an instance and open port 80
  2. sudo apt-get update
  3. sudo apt-get install nginx
  4. Use custom web resources placed inside nginx https://aws-tc-largeobjects.s3-us-west-2.amazonaws.com/ILT-TF-200-ACACAD-20-EN/Module-3-Challenge-Lab/static-website.zip
PLAINTEXT 
1. cd /var/www/html
2. sudo apt-get install wget
3. sudo apt-get install unzip 
4. cd /var/www
5. sudo chmod 777 html # grant permissions to the html directory
6. cd /var/www/html
7. wget {website-zip-url}
8. unzip {website-zip-file}
9. Refresh the page and you'll get an insecure http:// coffee shop website
1. cd /var/www/html 2. sudo apt-get install wget 3. sudo apt-get install unzip 4. cd /var/www 5. sudo chmod 777 html # grant permissions to the html directory 6. cd /var/www/html 7. wget {website-zip-url} 8. unzip {website-zip-file} 9. Refresh the page and you'll get an insecure http:// coffee shop website

item.title: arduino_Relay
item.description:
slug: arduino-Relay

Introduction to Arduino Relay

The Arduino Uno can only supply 5V or 3.3V DC to external devices. When you need higher voltages or AC power — for example, to control an electric fan or a desk lamp — you need a relay. Just like a switch, a relay lets you turn a circuit on or off from your program. If you want to build smart home appliances, a relay is absolutely essential!

item.title: Unreal Sequencer
item.description:
slug: Sequencer

Unreal Sequencer

Creating a Level Sequence A Level Sequence is the “container” for your cinematic scene. You must create one before you can start working in the Sequencer Editor. You can create a Level Sequence directly from the Toolbar under Cinematics (as shown below).

item.title: arduino_unreal
item.description:
slug: arduino-unreal

Arduino & Unreal

A plugin for controlling Arduino from Unreal Engine.

  1. Clone the repository and place it inside your Unreal project folder. https://github.com/RVillani/UE4Duino

Tutorial

  1. Set the port number to match your Arduino’s port number.
  2. Configure the USB port number for the connection.

Management

What is a Business?

A Business is formed by an individual or a group of people

Main activities

item.title: er_database
item.description:
slug: er-database

ER Database Normalization

Purpose — Leave a comment / Edit here

The purpose is to reduce data redundancy and avoid update anomalies.

item.title: software_engineer_at_google
item.description:
slug: software-engineer-at-google

Software Engineering at Google

Knowledge Sharing

  • Emphasizes knowledge sharing to avoid information silos. Every isolated silo gives rise to a problem, so building a knowledge base within the team is an important first step.
  • In team learning, building a psychologically safe environment for less experienced team members is important.

Team Interaction Patterns

Recommended Patterns Anti-Patterns
Basic questions or mistakes are guided in the right direction Basic questions are called out, and the person asking is punished
Explanations are given to help the questioner learn Explanations are given to show off
Responses are friendly, patient, and helpful Responses are arrogant, sarcastic, and unconstructive
Interactions are collaborative discussions aimed at finding solutions Interactions are arguments designed to produce “winners” and “losers”

Closing Thoughts

  • Start small: ask questions and write things down.
  • At the system level, encourage and reward those who take time to teach and expand their expertise for the benefit of others, their team, and the organization - not just for themselves.

How to Lead a Team?

Traditional managers worry about how to complete tasks, while great managers worry about what tasks will be completed (and trust their team to figure out how to solve them).

item.title: gcp
item.description:
slug: gcp

Last-Minute Prep - GCP Competition Notes

Notes - Concept Clarification

item.title: mqtt_setting
item.description:
slug: mqtt-setting

MQTT Basic Configuration

1. MQTT Broker Setup (Using Mosquitto)

INI 
# mosquitto.conf
listener 1883
allow_anonymous true
# mosquitto.conf listener 1883 allow_anonymous true

The hand-raise and head-turn counts will be stored directly into the database by the Python side.

item.title: the_Stranger
item.description:
slug: the-Stranger

Author’s Reflection

Although I move within a discipline I genuinely embrace, I continue to feel like an outsider, a consciousness misplaced in its own environment.
I watch people work with me, speak to me, and later turn away, grow resentful, fabricate stories.
This cycle has become the atmosphere in which I live — repetitive, inevitable.

item.title: hexo-noted
item.description:
slug: hexo-noted

HOW TO USE HEXO

insert image

PLAINTEXT 
![title](Realtive Path)
![title](Realtive Path)

run in local

PLAINTEXT 
hexo clean 
hexo generate
hexo server
hexo clean hexo generate hexo server

deploy

PLAINTEXT 
hexo c
hexo d
hexo c hexo d

item.title: My First Triathlon: Graduation Is Not the End, But a New Beginning
item.description:
slug: My-First-Triathlon-Graduation-Is-Not-the-End-But-a-New-Beginning

🏅 My First Triathlon: Graduation Is Not the End, But a New Beginning

by Ezra Wu | Jul 4, 2025

That was a 1 hour and 40 minutes challenge.

I participated in a triathlon for the first time in my life—running and rafting—on a hilly course. Halfway through, my body refused to cooperate, leaving only my willpower to push me forward.

item.title: The Coolest Thing I've Ever Done
item.description:
slug: The-Coolest-Thing-I-ve-Ever-Done

The Coolest Thing I’ve Ever Done: Building an AI Learning Tool for Visually Impaired Children

By Ezra Wu | April 5, 2025

The Beginning: A Spark of an Idea

In October 2024, I saw the announcement for the Microsoft Imagine Cup. A thought flashed through my mind: What if we could really use technology to change the world?

item.title: The Birth of Our Metaverse English Club
item.description:
slug: The-Birth-of-Our-Metaverse-English-Club

In 2024, I co-founded a club that quickly became one of my favorite projects: the Metaverse English Research Club. What makes this club special is its commitment to creating a fun, interactive, and immersive learning environment. We wanted to break the fear often associated with learning English, replacing it with curiosity and joy.

item.title: 20%–70% English Comprehension
item.description:
slug: eng-great

Learning Takes Patience

In Jordan’s teaching videos, the last slide mentions that the key to learning is patience.

Teacher Jordan told me: When I first started on 03.05.26, I could only understand 20–30% of what you said. But now I can understand 65–75% of what you say.

item.title: A is b, displacement
item.description:
slug: Diagrammatic-Design-Process-N-02

A is B?

Richards(1936) defines metaphor as the form “A is B”. a graph-like knowledge represents the individual memories with the nodes and arcs that are the ideas and the links between ideas respectively.

item.title: Can We Still Think Critically in the Age of AI?
item.description:
slug: mit-study-brain-chatgpt

A reflection inspired by the MIT study “Your Brain on ChatGPT.”

Recently, I started reading a large number of academic papers for my courses. While doing this, I became very curious about how we read and how we write. Should I search using Google and read original papers? Or should I rely on AI summaries?

item.title: dJOE - design Jigsaw On sitE A Computational Interface of Displacing Ideas in the Design Productive Process
item.description:
slug: Diagrammatic-Design-Process-N-djoe

dJOE: design Jigsaw On sitE

A Computational Interface of Displacing Ideas in the Design Productive Process

focus on how designer’s process of displacing ideas in the design productive phase and how to represent it on interface.

item.title: International Conference Information Visualisation (IV)
item.description:
slug: IV

similar theme

Huang, H.-Y. and T.-W. Chang (2017). Surfacing Repetitive Design Strategy with Visualization Tool. 2017 21st International Conference Information Visualisation (IV): 301–305. Wu, Y.-S. and T.-W. Chang (2018). Encoding the Convergent Path of Cross-Disciplinary Team. 2018 22nd International Conference Information Visualisation (IV): 438–441. Hsieh, T.-L. and T. W. Chang (2017). Whether the Relationscape of Interaction Design Strategies During Design Process Can Be Explained by Linkography. 2017 21st International Conference Information Visualisation (IV): 14–19. Liao, M. N. and T. W. Chang (2014). EVA: A Visualization Framework for Organizing Concept Sketches. 2014 18th International Conference on Information Visualisation: 398–401. Chang, T.-W., et al. (2023). Discussion on Preliminary Digital Assistance Mode in the Empathy Game Process of SPRINT Warm-Up. 2023 27th International Conference Information Visualisation (IV): 368–373. Chen, K.-C., et al. (2023). Relational Structure Visualization in Composition. 2023 27th International Conference Information Visualisation (IV): 23–28. Li, J.-R., et al. (2023). Constructing a Cross-Disciplinary Idea Convergence System Using AIGC: A Case Study of Engineering and Design. 2023 27th International Conference Information Visualisation (IV): 352–357.

item.title: Looking Back, Everything from That Year Became an Unrepeatable Memory
item.description:
slug: if

It has already been 1.5 years into graduate school life, which means 2 years remain until graduation. 2 years left to chase my HCI research dream and go on adventures.

I cannot foresee who I will become in the future, but I know — I simply love living in the present moment.

Practicing small talk with a teacher from the Philippines — a skill he spent 10 years mastering. Learning Taiwanese and Thai alongside my Thai friends. Dreaming with my Chinese friends of one day creating something new together. Exploring design iteration with the professors and students at SoftLab. Staying up late with senior lab mates, writing papers — savoring what it means to be the junior. Embracing the ocean, exploring sea slugs all by myself. Making peace with my past self, and embracing the unknown. Accepting every change that arrives in my life.

item.title: PLAYING JIGSAW & One Map Many Ideas
item.description:
slug: Diagrammatic-Design-Process-N-01

One Map Many Ideas : How Novice Designers Generate Conceptual Designs From An Idea Map

ICF model

DIM creates a concept map made of idea entities (ie) and the links between them. Each idea entity follows the ICF model (Issue, Concept, Form) and contains three attributes:

item.title: Visualising the Design Process with Dynamic Graph
item.description:
slug: Diagrammatic-Design-Process-N-03

Visualising the Design Process with Dynamic Graph

concept

Designers have often forgotten after the completion of the design, but throughout viewing these recorded graphics can evoke their memory, and provide more inspiration to and thoughts.
